aliases: Kru' Naing' Rob Rom' Hak Tiex Praog
description: The United Wa State Army is the military wing of the United Wa State Party, the de facto ruling party of Wa State in Myanmar. Formed in 1989 after the collapse of the Communist Party of Burma, it is a well-equipped ethnic minority army of 20,000–30,000 soldiers led by Bao Youxiang. Despite being de facto independent, it officially recognizes Myanmar's sovereignty and has signed ceasefire and peace deals. It is the largest non-state armed group in Myanmar and leads the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee.

country_links.evidence_quote: displacing traditional black market sources in South East Asia such as Thailand and Cambodia.
country_links.context: Cambodia was a traditional black market source of arms for the UWSA.

country_links.evidence_quote: The UWSA is supported by China , who gives the UWSA more support than to the Myanmar government.
country_links.context: The UWSA receives support, weapons, and training from China, and China helped establish the FPNCC alliance.

country_links.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army also works with the ULFA -ATF, an Assam resistance group which launches attacks in India through mobile bases in Myanmar.
country_links.context: The UWSA works with the ULFA-ATF, an Assam resistance group that launches attacks in India from Myanmar bases.

country_links.evidence_quote: the trafficking of drugs to neighbouring Thailand and Laos.
country_links.context: The UWSA traffics drugs to Laos.

country_links.evidence_quote: the trafficking of drugs to neighbouring Thailand and Laos.
country_links.context: The UWSA traffics drugs to Thailand and has tensions over border encroachment.
